mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates above 5% increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing equity markets to price in lower future earnings and produce negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in >70% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.1) in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength across consumer discretionary and fixed investment channels, driving corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series diverges, or quarterly forward returns are negative in >60% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month momentum in the index of industrial production reflects immediate capacity utilization and output growth, triggering upward earnings revisions that push S&P 500 prices higher over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no directional bias (win rate ~50%) during recovery phases, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when inflation exceeds 4%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of restrictive policy rates above 4.5% and monthly nonfarm payrolls additions below 100,000 creates a policy-induced demand contraction that suppresses equity valuations and yields negative forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >65% of months meeting both conditions, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings exceeding 0.4% signal persistent price pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy and compress corporate profit margins, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in >70% of months with CPIAUCSL MoM > 0.4%, or the signal's out-of-sample Sharpe ratio is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls provides a stable foundation for consumer spending and corporate revenue, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter through fundamental earnings support.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative in >60% of periods with positive PAYEMS growth, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.01 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 1.5% indicates a decelerating economic expansion that reduces business investment and consumer confidence, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of quarters where GDPC1 growth drops below 1.5%, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark in walk-forward tests.
